channel.title: Drupal Canvas
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Canvas.

item.title: #3590948: Add TMGMT translation integration for content entities
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3590948 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 1

Summary

Site owners using the Translation Management Tool (TMGMT) to translate content entities (nodes, Canvas pages, etc.) that contain component_tree fields can now see and edit each component instance's translatable props as individual strings in the TMGMT job item review form. Previously, without a field processor, TMGMT treated the entire component_tree field as an opaque blob, making content translation impractical. The change also ensures the TMGMT review form correctly loads CKEditor 5 for rich-text component props.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ComponentTreeFieldProcessor TMGMT field processor and ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor shared service added to the Canvas module.

Technical details

The new ComponentTreeFieldProcessor class in src/Tmgmt/ComponentTreeFieldProcessor.php extends DefaultFieldProcessor from tmgmt_content. Its extractTranslatableData() method converts each ComponentTreeItem's inputs JSON property into a ComponentInputsMapping typed config object and delegates to ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor to discover translatable props via config schema metadata. setTranslations() writes accepted translations back into the correct nested positions in the raw inputs array using a recursive walker (writeNestedTranslationsToInputs()), necessary because component source props can have translatable values at arbitrary nesting depth with no field item to delegate to. The processor is registered via TmgmtHooks::fieldInfoAlter() by setting tmgmt_field_processor on the component_tree field info entry when tmgmt_content is installed.

The extraction logic previously embedded in ComponentInputsConfigProcessor was extracted into a new ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor service so it can be shared between both the config-entity processor (ComponentInputsConfigProcessor) and the new content-entity processor. ComponentInputsConfigProcessor is now a thin wrapper around this service. As part of this refactor, TmgmtHooks takes over the hook_config_schema_info_alter registration of ComponentInputsConfigProcessor that previously lived in canvas_dev_translation's ConfigTranslationSupportHooks.

tmgmt_job_item_edit_form is added to the list of form IDs that use static prop sources in ReduxIntegratedFieldWidgetsHooks, ensuring Redux-integrated field widgets initialise correctly inside the TMGMT review form. A filter in ComponentInputsTranslatablesExtractor works around a TMGMT upstream bug (#3601246) where a translatable type: ignore element with an array #text value would crash later TMGMT processing.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 19, 2026

Key contributors

  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • isholgueras (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591684: UI Exception / 500 Error when triggering Preview on Content Templates causes persistent state lock
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591684 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

When editing Content Templates in the Canvas page builder, the Preview button appeared in the toolbar even when no preview entity had been selected. Clicking it caused a 500 Internal Server Error, and clicking "Exit Preview" afterward left the editor stuck in a broken layout state that required a hard browser refresh to clear. Both error conditions are now resolved: the Preview button is hidden for Content Templates until a preview entity is chosen, and the preview flow uses the correct backend endpoint when a preview entity is available.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The 500 error originated in ComponentTreeLoader::getCanvasFieldName(), which throws a LogicException if the entity passed to it is not a canvas_page entity type. The generic layout GET route /canvas/api/v0/layout/{entity_type}/{entity} routes through this check, so calling it with a Content Template entity inevitably fails. A regression had caused PreviewControls.tsx to display the Preview button inside the template editor context even when no previewEntityId param was present in the URL, and its "enter preview" click handler navigated to a path that PagePreview.tsx backed with the wrong API queries.

The fix has three coordinated parts. First, PreviewControls.tsx now returns null when isTemplateContext && !previewEntityId, hiding the button until a preview entity is selected in the ContentPreviewSelector dropdown. Second, when a previewEntityId is set, the click handler navigates to /preview/template/${entityType}/${bundle}/${previewEntityId}/${viewMode}, and "Exit Preview" uses the isTemplatePreviewRoute branch to navigate back to /template/${entityType}/${bundle}/${viewMode}/${entityId}, keeping the editor's Redux EditorFrameContext state consistent. Third, PagePreview.tsx detects the /preview/template/ prefix via a local isContentTemplate flag, skips both useGetPageLayoutQuery and the postPreview mutation (both of which use the generic layout endpoint), and instead drives the preview iframe via useGetSnapshotPreviewQuery with isTemplate: true. That query calls the dedicated route /canvas/api/v0/layout-content-template/${entityType}.${bundle}.${viewMode}/${previewEntityId}, which is designed to handle non-canvas_page entities. The useTemplateRef hook centralises the two context signals (isTemplateContext from Redux, isTemplatePreviewRoute from the URL path) so both PreviewControls and other components can branch consistently.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 16, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • mayur-sose
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • hooroomoo (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591584: Publishing a canvas_page via auto-save API drops all non-default-language translations
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591584 · 3 contributors · 10 comments

Summary

Sites running the Canvas module with multilingual content were silently losing translations whenever a page was published through the Canvas editor's auto-save flow. Every non-default-language translation would disappear from the published page. This critical data-loss bug is now fixed: publishing via the auto-save API preserves all translations correctly. A related edge case where the content_moderation field was being ignored during auto-save publish is also corrected.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: AutoSaveManager::isPersistedComputedField(FieldDefinitionInterface $field_definition): bool added as a public static method to identify computed fields that are user-editable and persisted outside the entity's own tables (currently path and moderation_state).

Technical details

The root cause was in ApiAutoSaveController's publish path. The auto-save snapshot only ever captures the single translation being actively edited. Previously, the controller saved the entity reconstructed directly from that snapshot, which discarded every other translation. The fix loads the authoritative stored entity via loadUnchanged(), identifies which translation the snapshot belongs to (via its langcode), and then applies only the changed fields from the snapshot onto that specific translation of the loaded entity. All other translations are untouched because the real entity is never discarded.

This approach also fixes a subtler secondary bug: content_translation's FieldTranslationSynchronizer::synchronizeFields() requires a valid loaded revision ID to determine the correct synchronization source for untranslatable ("symmetric") field columns. The reconstructed entity lacked this, causing the synchronizer to fall back to a stale translation and potentially reverting structural edits on the default language. Loading the real entity avoids this.

A new public static method, AutoSaveManager::isPersistedComputedField(FieldDefinitionInterface $field_definition), was extracted to identify computed fields that are user-editable and persisted outside the entity's own tables: the path field (persisted as path_alias entities via PathItem::postSave()) and moderation_state (persisted as content_moderation_state entities). These fields must be carried through the auto-save store and applied on publish; previously only path was special-cased. The issue notes that no reliable runtime signal exists to detect these fields generically and links #3503446 as the follow-up to remove the hardcoding.

An earlier approach that re-added missing translations back onto the reconstructed entity after the fact was explicitly rejected because it papered over the dropped translations without fixing the symmetric structural-edit corruption.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 23, 2026

Key contributors

  • narendrar (Acquia)
  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • tim.plunkett (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • laro
  • bnjmnm (Acquia)
  • f.mazeikis

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3589076: Detect conflicts for Page entities during the handling of auto-saves/pending calls
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +52/−3 · php +35/−3 · js +17
  • Discussion: #3589076 · 2 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

In the Canvas module, auto-save entries were being stored using $entity->toArray(), which includes computed fields. Computed fields are derived at runtime and are not actually stored, so including them in the auto-save snapshot could cause spurious hash mismatches and incorrect conflict signals during the auto-saves/pending polling cycle. This MR fixes the serialization step so that computed fields are excluded from auto-save storage and hash comparisons. The one exception is the path field type, which Drupal marks as computed but does store, so it is intentionally kept. Cypress tests are also updated to assert that polling the auto-saves/pending endpoint continues to return HTTP 200 and not unexpected error codes.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

A new private static method AutoSaveManager::toStorableArray() replaces the direct $entity->toArray() calls used in two places: saveEntity() (where the snapshot and data_hash are written) and normalizeEntity() (where the entity is diffed for conflicts). For FieldableEntityInterface entities the method iterates getFields(include_computed: TRUE) and skips any field whose definition returns isComputed() === true, unless the field item list is an instance of PathFieldItemList. The path field is special-cased because \Drupal\path\Hook\PathHooks::entityBaseFieldInfo marks it computed even though it is persisted. For non-fieldable entities the method falls back to $entity->toArray() unchanged. Without this fix, any computed field value that happens to differ between two entity loads (e.g. cache-warmed or lazily resolved fields) would produce a different hash and trigger a false conflict detection in the broader conflict-detection work being built in this issue series.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 7, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582558: Symmetrically translatable config-defined component trees, STEP 4: allow translating component instance inputs via TMGMT for ContentTemplates & PageRegions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 13 files, +833/−200 · php +814/−199 · yml +12/−1 · neon +6 · json +1
  • Discussion: #3582558 · 6 contributors · 24 comments
  • Followers: 10

Summary

Canvas site owners who use TMGMT (Translation Management Tool) to manage config-translation workflows can now translate the text inputs of component instances stored in ContentTemplate and PageRegion config entities. Inputs populated by static prop sources (plain strings, rich text, URIs, and multi-value sequences) are exposed as translatable segments in the TMGMT job UI. Inputs driven by entity field references or host-entity URL prop sources are correctly excluded. Optional inputs not populated in the default-language template can still receive a translation value. This is Step 4 of a series of issues making Canvas config-defined component trees symmetrically translatable; it requires the canvas_dev_translation module to be enabled.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New translatable_uri config schema type added; field.value.link and field.value.uri URI properties retyped to translatable_uri via hook_config_schema_info_alter() in canvas_dev_translation.
  • New API: New ComponentInputsConfigProcessor class in Drupal\canvas\Tmgmt\ (marked @internal) registered as tmgmt_config_processor for canvas.content_template.*.*.* and canvas.page_region.* config schema types.

Technical details

The central addition is ComponentInputsConfigProcessor (Drupal\canvas\Tmgmt\, @internal), which extends TMGMT's DefaultConfigProcessor. It is registered on the canvas.content_template.*.*.* and canvas.page_region.* config schema types via a new hook_config_schema_info_alter() implementation in canvas_dev_translation's ConfigTranslationSupportHooks.

When extractTranslatables() encounters a ComponentInputsMapping element, it bypasses standard schema-driven extraction and instead loads the Component entity and its JsonSchemaPropsComponentSourceBase component source. It then constructs an ad-hoc concrete schema by mapping each prop's field type to the corresponding field.value.* config schema definition, handling four cardinality/property-count combinations (single cardinality + single property, multi-cardinality + single property, single cardinality + multi-property, multi-cardinality + multi-property). Props not backed by a StaticPropSource are emitted as type: ignore. Optional inputs absent from the default translation are injected as NULL into $config_data so TMGMT's ArrayElement::getAllKeys() does not silently skip them; because TMGMT's flattener drops empty strings, the Unicode sentinel ∅ ("empty set") is substituted to force these inputs visible in the translation UI, via the private extractTranslatablesIncludingEmpty() method.

Two config schema alterations accompany the processor. field.value.link's uri property and field.value.uri's value property are retyped to a new translatable_uri schema type (a translatable variant of type: uri) added by the MR with a @todo referencing core issue #3595275.

A bug fix in CanvasStaticPropSourceFieldWidget::setConfig() was also required to make TMGMT's save path work correctly. An assert() requiring values to be delta-keyed lists was removed (TMGMT does not supply delta keys); multi-value fields now strip only the add_more pseudo-key. A second bug was fixed where the config override was written to $base_key instead of $subkey for multi-property field types, and optional empty properties in the base config now skip creating an override rather than writing an empty value.

Functional test coverage is split into two new parallel test classes sharing a ConfigWithComponentTreeTranslationTestBase fixture: ConfigWithComponentTreeConfigTranslationUiTest (core Config Translation UI) and ConfigWithComponentTreeTmgmtUiTest (TMGMT UI). Both assert the same resulting LanguageConfigOverride, covering plain prose, rich text (value-only override, format excluded), URI-esque props, multi-cardinality sequences, optional unpopulated props, and excluded non-static prop sources.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 1, 2026

Key contributors

  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

Collaboration

tedbow opened two early MRs (!833, !885) demonstrating TMGMT translation working for plain-string inputs, with screenshots. wim leers noted in comment #9 that the approach could only ever surface plain strings: multi-property field types like link could not work because TMGMT's Data::wordCount assumed a flat string, and the default ConfigProcessorInterface had no way to handle the value/format split of formatted text or the uri/options split of links. This diagnosis ruled out the earlier MRs and established that a custom ConfigProcessorInterface implementation was the only viable path. wim leers closed both MRs and opened !1099, which resolved the remaining crash by removing the incorrect delta assertion in CanvasStaticPropSourceFieldWidget::setConfig() and building the full custom processor.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591638: Add a computed `src` field property to the image field type
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591638 · 2 contributors · 6 comments

Summary

Canvas's image field type now exposes a src field property that code-component developers can use to bind an image URL to a component prop. Previously the only browser-ready URL was named src_with_alternate_widths, which leaked an internal implementation detail into the data-selection UI. The new src is a clean alias: it resolves to the same URL (including the ?alternateWidths query string needed for Canvas's responsive-image handling) but is presented with a name that is meaningful to component authors. Automatic shape matching and content-template suggestions are unaffected and continue to resolve src_with_alternate_widths.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Image field type in Canvas now exposes a computed src property (alias of src_with_alternate_widths) for direct use in code-component data selection.

Technical details

The change is in ImageItemOverride::propertyDefinitions(). After the existing src_with_alternate_widths property is constructed (a computed DataDefinition backed by ComputedUrlWithQueryString), a new src property is added by cloning it and calling setSetting('is source for', 'src_with_alternate_widths') on the clone. The is source for setting is the same mechanism already used in TextWithSummaryItemOverride to exclude raw value/format properties in favour of processed. In EntityFieldPropSourceMatcher, any property carrying is source for is skipped during shape matching iteration (continue), which keeps src out of automatic prop matching while still making it available for explicit selection. Both src and src_with_alternate_widths produce identical dependency graphs, as confirmed by the test case at line 1011 of PropExpressionTest. The @todo on this property points to #3464003, which tracks replacing computed field properties with adapters; that change will require an update path for any component trees that consume src.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 8, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • lauriii (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3574857: Add client-side transform for entity_reference_autocomplete to ReduxIntegratedFieldWidgetsHooks::fieldWidgetInfoAlter
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 9 files, +386/−6 · js +374/−5 · php +7/−1 · yml +4 · md +1
  • Discussion: #3574857 · 9 contributors · 42 comments
  • Followers: 11

Summary

The Canvas editor now supports the entity_reference_autocomplete field widget when used as a component prop. Previously, selecting an entity via the autocomplete field would not produce a usable value in the Canvas prop system. With this change, the widget is registered as supported and the selected autocomplete value is correctly transformed into a numeric entity ID before being stored.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New entityAutocompleteTargetId client-side transform registered in Canvas; available for use in custom Canvas field widget integrations.

Technical details

On the PHP side, ReduxIntegratedFieldWidgetsHooks::fieldWidgetInfoAlter() gains an entry for entity_reference_autocomplete with two transforms: mainProperty (pointing to target_id) and the new entityAutocompleteTargetId. The FieldWidgetSupportTest completion metric advances from 15/28 to 16/28.

On the JavaScript side, a new entityAutocompleteTargetId transformer is added in ui/src/utils/transforms.ts. It extracts the numeric ID from Drupal's autocomplete display format ("Label (id)") using ENTITY_AUTOCOMPLETE_MATCH = /.+\s\(([^)]+)\)\s*$/, which mirrors \Drupal\Core\Entity\Element\EntityAutocomplete::extractEntityIdFromAutocompleteInput. For comma-separated tags-mode input (multiple references in a single field), a quote-aware splitAutocompleteValues function mirrors Drupal.autocomplete.splitValues so labels containing commas (e.g. "Hello, world" (3)) are handled correctly. The transform is idempotent: if the value is already a bare ID (because the transform ran on a prior commit cycle), it passes through unchanged rather than being dropped. Invalid entries that do not match the autocomplete pattern also pass through as bare IDs so that a single bad entry does not discard the entire selection; server-side validation surfaces the actual error.

shouldSkipPropValidation in componentFieldValidation is extended to skip client-side JSON schema validation for props whose schema type is object or array of objects, because their source shape differs from the resolved value and cannot be validated client-side. A new canvas.transform.entityAutocompleteTargetId Drupal library is declared in canvas.libraries.yml. This work follows the same pattern used for daterange_default in #3522130.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 20, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • scott falconer (Acquia)
  • bnjmnm (Acquia)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • jibran
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • hooroomoo (Acquia)

Collaboration

wim leers opened the issue and reviewed the first MR promptly, pointing out that the initial unit tests only verified array wiring and did not test any actual transform behavior, and specifying the shape of coverage needed. After scott falconer reworked the MR with a real client-side parser and behavior-focused tests, wim leers approved but then identified two further problems via inline MR notes: the non-trivial regex had no explanation, and one test case appeared to document an expected hard failure in production. scott falconer addressed both by adding a plain-English comment on ENTITY_AUTOCOMPLETE_MATCH with @see references to core, and by changing the transform to return null for invalid input rather than passing the raw label through. A subsequent manual testing attempt by penyaskito surfaced a failure that turned out to be caused by a separate regression (#3588884) rather than this MR, and after that regression was resolved the MR was cleared for merge.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591640: Selects based on enums defined in schema.json not working because id not a supported key in schema or registering duplicated JSON Schema in Ajv
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591640 · 1 contributor · 5 comments

Summary

Selecting an option from a Canvas component's enum/select field (for example a heading level or column width) threw a JavaScript error and the change never applied, leaving the preview frozen. This happened because the PHP justinrainbow/json-schema library injects id or $id keys when it resolves $ref references in a component's schema.json, and when those keys reached the front-end Ajv validator they caused either a "NOT SUPPORTED: keyword id, use $id" error or a "schema already exists" duplicate-registration error. The fix strips id and $id from the resolved schema before it is sent to the browser, so Ajv receives a clean schema and select interactions work correctly again.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The method stripNonStandardJsonSchemaKeys in JsonSchemaPropsComponentSourceBase recursively removed Canvas-specific extension keys (meta:enum, x-translation-context) from prop schemas before they were serialised into the field_data payload sent to the React UI. The PHP library justinrainbow/json-schema populates id (Draft 4) and $id (Draft 7) on every object it produces when resolving $refs against schema.json files; these keys were not being stripped. Ajv on the client side rejected id entirely in strict mode, and when multiple props referenced the same shared definition (e.g. json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/column-width), the same $id value was registered with Ajv more than once, producing a duplicate-schema error.

The fix renames the method to simplifySchemaForAjvClient and adds 'id' and '$id' to the $keys_to_remove list. The stripping is recursive, so nested object and array sub-schemas are also cleaned. Test fixture expected-data arrays throughout JsComponentTest and SingleDirectoryComponentTest are updated to omit the $id values that were previously present in the assertions. A new Playwright end-to-end test case exercises editComponentProp with a select input type to confirm that enum props update the preview correctly after the fix.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 8, 2026

Key contributors

  • bnjmnm (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • isholgueras (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3583684: Symmetric translations for content entities' component tree fields should allow only translatable keys in `inputs` to be overridden
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +60/−0 · md +60
  • Discussion: #3583684 · 5 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 10

Summary

Canvas now correctly handles symmetric content translation for content entities with component tree fields. Previously, saving a non-default-language translation that omitted non-translatable component inputs (such as cta1href or attributes in an SDC hero component) caused a LogicException or EntityStorageException about required properties. The module now stores all inputs on every translation row and synchronizes non-translatable input values from the default translation automatically at write time, so translators and developers do not need to duplicate non-translatable data in each translation.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Drupal's content_translation.synchronizer (FieldTranslationSynchronizerInterface) handles synchronizing non-translatable field column groups, including Canvas's tree group (uuid, parent_uuid, slot, component_id, component_version). Non-translatable keys inside the inputs JSON blob property of ComponentTreeItem are not a column group and therefore fall outside that mechanism, causing ComponentTreeItem::preSave() to throw because required inputs were absent.

The fix introduces ComponentTreeFieldSymmetricalTranslationSynchronizer, registered in CanvasServiceProvider as a Symfony service decorator on content_translation.synchronizer. After the standard column-group sync, the decorator's synchronizeComponentInstanceInputs() iterates every component tree field in symmetric mode (tree synced, inputs translatable), determines translatable keys via ComponentInputs::getTranslatableInputKeys() (which calls ComponentInputs::resolveConfigSchemaMapping() to read config schema translatability markers), then writes array_merge(target_inputs, non_translatable_from_default) back to each non-default translation's field item. This ensures non-translatable inputs from the default translation always win while preserving the translation's own translatable values.

A special case handles net-new component instances added to the default translation: because the target translation has no prior inputs for that instance (core's createMergedItem() may have placed inputs from a different component instance at that delta), the decorator uses the default translation's full input set instead of the (incorrect) target inputs. Rendering, JSON:API, and the inputs_resolved computed property all read inputs identically for any translation with no merge logic at read time. MR !1219 adds ADR #12 to docs/adr/ documenting this write-time synchronization architecture and its tradeoffs (including that translatable inputs on newly added component instances are intentionally left empty in non-default translations, requiring manual translator input).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 8, 2026

Key contributors

  • tedbow (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

Collaboration

In comment #17, wim leers raised questions about the fundamentals of the MR approach after reviewing, which prompted a synchronous chat with tedbow that produced a simplified implementation plan. Earlier (comment #9), wim leers flagged that the parallel config-entity translation issue (#3582478) should provide a centralized API for determining translatable inputs so the logic would not need to be duplicated for content and config entity cases.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591633: Move CLI sync settings to canvas.config.json, enable everything by default
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591633 · 1 contributor · 4 comments

Summary

The Canvas CLI push and pull commands now read their sync preferences (whether to include pages, content templates, and global regions) from a sync key in canvas.config.json rather than from environment variables. All three content types are now enabled by default, so a project with no sync key set will automatically include pages, content templates, and regions in every push/pull operation. The old CANVAS_INCLUDE_PAGES, CANVAS_INCLUDE_CONTENT_TEMPLATES, and CANVAS_INCLUDE_REGIONS environment variables continue to work but emit deprecation warnings and the CLI offers to migrate them to canvas.config.json automatically.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New optional sync key in canvas.config.json controls whether pages, contentTemplates, and regions are included in CLI push/pull; all three default to true.
  • Deprecation: CANVAS_INCLUDE_PAGES, CANVAS_INCLUDE_CONTENT_TEMPLATES, CANVAS_INCLUDE_REGIONS environment variables and --include-pages/--include-content-templates/--include-regions CLI flags deprecated in favour of canvas.config.json sync settings and --no-* flags.

Technical details

The sync object is now the canonical source of truth for per-content-type inclusion. It is read from canvas.config.json by readConfiguredSyncSettings() in packages/cli/src/config.ts, with the DEFAULT_CANVAS_CONFIG.sync object in packages/discovery/src/config.ts providing { pages: true, contentTemplates: true, regions: true } as defaults. If a sync key is absent from canvas.config.json, the defaults apply; if it is present, each key overrides the corresponding default.

The legacy env vars are handled by LEGACY_SYNC_ENV_SETTINGS and the applySyncOptionAliasesAndWarnings migration helper. When the env vars are still set, the CLI still honors them (feeding them through setConfig) but warns the user and, if confirmed interactively, writes the equivalent sync.* keys back to canvas.config.json. The --include-pages, --include-content-templates, and --include-regions CLI flags have similarly been deprecated in favor of negative flags (--no-pages, --no-content-templates, --no-regions) for disabling sync at runtime, because the positive forms are now no-ops when everything defaults to enabled.

Upgrade

Update canvas.config.json to include the sync key and remove the old environment variables:

// canvas.config.json
{
  "sync": {
    "pages": true,
    "contentTemplates": true,
    "regions": true
  }
}

Remove these environment variables from .env and CI pipelines (they are now deprecated):

CANVAS_INCLUDE_PAGES
CANVAS_INCLUDE_CONTENT_TEMPLATES
CANVAS_INCLUDE_REGIONS

To selectively disable sync for one content type, set the value to false in canvas.config.json, or pass --no-pages / --no-content-templates / --no-regions on the command line. The previously preferred flags --include-pages=false etc. are deprecated.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 7, 2026

Key contributors

  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • lauriii (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591628: On Drupal 11.3, Components are not regenerated when configuration changes (e.g. creating a media type)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591628 · 2 contributors · 5 comments

Summary

On Drupal 11.3+, Canvas Components were not regenerated when site configuration changed (for example, adding a new media type) unless a full cache rebuild was performed first. This meant that widgets expected to reflect the new configuration (such as the media library selector on an image prop) would remain stale until drush cr was run. The fix resolves the issue for fresh installs and any install sequence where canvas_stark is installed before Canvas has been used for the first time.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Since Drupal 11.3, ThemeInstaller calls DrupalKernel::updateThemes() when a theme is installed or uninstalled, which rebuilds the entire service container. Canvas's module install hook installs canvas_stark partway through, triggering that container rebuild. After that point, the ComponentSourceHooks service object — and all services it had injected, including ComponentSourceManager and PersistentPropShapeRepository — are orphaned instances pointing at the pre-rebuild container. Any prop shapes written to the orphaned PersistentPropShapeRepository are lost because its ::destruct() is never called; the live container's repository starts cold, so Components are never persistently generated.

The fix injects DrupalKernelInterface into ComponentSourceHooks using #[Autowire(service: 'kernel')]. In the module install hook path (hook_install), instead of calling $this->rebuild() (which uses the stale injected $this->componentSourceManager), the code now calls $this->kernel->getContainer()->get(ComponentSourceManager::class) to obtain the live post-rebuild instance, then calls generateComponents() on that. This ensures prop shapes land in the cache that belongs to the live container and will actually outlive the request.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 5, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591624: Block validation is broken for any block without a default value for `label_display`
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591624 · 4 contributors · 9 comments

Summary

A critical bug in the Canvas module caused block validation to fail for any block plugin that did not provide a default value for label_display in its configuration. This meant such blocks were incorrectly excluded from the Canvas component library and could not be used as Canvas components. The bug was introduced by the stricter block validation added in MR #781 and was breaking all Drupal CMS CI pipelines. Sites using Canvas with block plugins that rely on the inherited default for label_display were affected.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The bug was introduced in BlockComponentDiscovery::checkRequirements() when MR #781 added stricter block settings validation. That method constructs a dummy config array to pass to TypedConfigManagerInterface::createFromNameAndData() for the block.settings.* schema, so it can validate the block plugin's default configuration. The dummy array included 'label_display' => FALSE (a PHP boolean), but the block.settings.* config schema requires label_display to be the string '0' (representing "hidden") rather than a boolean. Passing FALSE produced a type violation, causing the validation to report errors for every block that did not explicitly override label_display in defaultConfiguration(). The correct value '0' was already used elsewhere in the same file at the point where real component metadata is built (line 169 of BlockComponentDiscovery.php). The fix brings the dummy config in checkRequirements() in line with that existing correct usage by changing 'label_display' => FALSE to 'label_display' => '0'.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 4, 2026

Key contributors

  • pameeela (Technocrat)
  • jibran (Morpht)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590572: Indicate translation availability per language in the Canvas language switcher dropdown
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +99/−5 · js +46/−4 · yml +25 · css +17 · php +11/−1
  • Discussion: #3590572 · 5 contributors · 7 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Site editors and content authors using the Canvas editor on multilingual Drupal sites will now see a checkmark icon next to each language in the Canvas language switcher dropdown that has a dedicated translation for the current entity. Languages without a translation show no indicator, making it immediately clear that selecting them will display a default-language fallback rather than a dedicated translation. The goal is not to hide untranslated languages (since other referenced entities may still render differently), but to communicate translation state clearly.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New translations.available array of language codes added to the ApiLayoutController::get() JSON response.

Technical details

The ApiLayoutController::get() method is extended to append a translations key to its JSON response. It calls getTranslationLanguages() on the entity (guarded by method_exists to safely handle non-translatable entity types), extracts language codes with array_keys(), and returns them as translations.available (an array of language code strings). The OpenAPI spec in openapi.yaml is updated to document the new translations object, which is added to both the path-level response schema and the reusable component schema.

On the frontend, LayoutModelSliceState in layoutModelSlice.ts gains a translations field typed as Record<string, any>, with a new selectTranslations selector. The setInitialLayoutModel reducer stores the translations payload from the API response. The LanguageSelect component reads this via useAppSelector(selectTranslations) and, for each language option, renders a CheckIcon (from @radix-ui/react-icons) in a fixed-width container when the language code appears in translations.available. A data-canvas-has-translation="true" attribute is also set on the language name text element for Playwright test targeting; the new end-to-end test assertions verify that translated languages (English, French) carry this attribute while untranslated ones (Spanish) do not. The filterLayoutForPost helper in the PHP test base is updated to strip translations from POST payloads, and kernel tests assert that the translations.available array is present in GET responses.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • bnjmnm (Acquia)

Collaboration

tedbow noted in a review comment that the parent issue #3585452 (Create a preview mode for non-default languages) needs to be committed before this work can land, flagging a hard dependency between the two issues.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591685: Config schema validation rejects existing inputs on Fallback components after their source module/theme is removed
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +9/−65 · php +7/−58 · yml +2/−7
  • Diff: e220bbd
  • Discussion: #3591685 · 2 contributors · 4 comments

Summary

Two fixes shipped in this commit for the Multiple Email module. First, the multiple_email.confirmer service was converted to use Symfony autowiring, so it no longer requires a long list of manually specified arguments in the service container. Second, a bug in the add-email form was corrected: the redirect to the email management page now only fires after the email entity has been successfully saved, rather than unconditionally at the end of the submit handler.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In multiple_email.services.yml, the multiple_email.confirmer service received autowire: true. The previous definition listed seven explicit arguments (@config.factory, @datetime.time, @plugin.manager.mail, @language_manager, @token, @logger.channel.multiple_email, @string_translation). After the change, only $logger: '@logger.channel.multiple_email' remains explicit, because a named logger channel cannot be resolved by type alone; all other dependencies are injected automatically. A test-layer stub file that declared a local LoggerChannelInterface shim was removed along with this change.

In MultipleEmailAddForm::submitForm(), the $form_state->setRedirect('multiple_email.manage', ...) call was moved inside the conditional block that wraps the $entity->save() and confirm calls. Previously it sat outside that block, so it executed regardless of whether the save branch was entered. Two unused use Drupal\user\UserInterface imports were also removed from the entity class and its interface.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 16, 2026
  • Committed: May 23, 2026 (24 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • tommynin
  • freelock (Freelock)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • pameeela (Technocrat)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591642: LogicException on content templates with dynamic field bindings after upgrading from 1.4.1 to 1.5.1
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +15/−1 · php +15/−1
  • Diff: 6cdb6d7
  • Discussion: #3591642 · 2 contributors · 13 comments

Summary

The sections listing page in Bootstrap UI Kit now shows a "View" button alongside the existing "Manage" button for each section. Clicking "View" opens the UI Kit preview page scrolled to the relevant section in a new browser tab, letting site builders quickly check a section's appearance without leaving the listing.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The change is in SectionsListForm. Previously the operations column held only the $manage_link render element. The patch adds a second link built with Url::fromRoute('bootstrap_ui_kit.ui_kit', [], ['fragment' => $section_key]), wrapped in a Link::fromTextAndUrl() call and rendered as a button with target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer". Both links are now nested inside a #type => 'container' element with class operations, which replaces the bare $manage_link in the table row data array.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 9, 2026
  • Committed: May 22, 2026 (17 days later)

Key contributors

  • tommynin
  • freelock (Freelock)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • pameeela (Technocrat)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591623: 1.5.0: Autosave triggers repeated 409 conflict errors on non-English monolingual sites
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical unknown (0)
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +0/−228 · yml +0/−22 · php +0/−206
  • Diff: 8dac25f
  • Discussion: #3591623 · 2 contributors · 11 comments

Summary

The entity_blueprint module's workspace-enforcement settings have been completely removed. The require_workspace option (which defaulted to true, blocking entity saves outside an active workspace) and its admin settings page at /admin/config/content/entity-blueprint no longer exist. Sites that had explicitly set this option to false to permit saves in the live environment will lose that configuration on the next config import. The change was made in response to the Canvas autosave issue where enforcing workspace context caused repeated false 409 conflict errors on non-English monolingual sites.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: entity_blueprint.settings config object removed; the require_workspace key no longer exists and any stored value will be orphaned.

Technical details

The commit deletes config/install/entity_blueprint.settings.yml (holding the require_workspace: true default), config/schema/entity_blueprint.schema.yml (the schema for that config object), the route definition (entity_blueprint.settings), the menu link, src/Form/SettingsForm.php, src/Form/SettingsFormTrait.php, and src/Hook/SettingsFormAlter.php. The SettingsFormAlter hook was wiring entity_blueprint settings into a navigation_plus settings form via hook_form_navigation_plus_settings_alter; that integration is also gone. After this change there is no entity_blueprint.settings config object and no means to toggle workspace enforcement through entity_blueprint's own UI or through navigation_plus.

Upgrade

If your site had entity_blueprint.settings in configuration management (e.g. config/sync/entity_blueprint.settings.yml), remove that file before running drush config:import, otherwise the import will error on an unknown config object. No code substitution is needed; the workspace enforcement feature is simply removed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 3, 2026
  • Committed: May 22, 2026 (12 days later)

Key contributors

  • f.mazeikis
  • f0ns
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • tommynin
  • interx (Anvil)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591629: PropShape strips only `id`, not `$id`, so `$ref`-based components (e.g. image) are not generated for JSON Schema Draft-07 (with `justinrainbow/json-schema` 6.9.0)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 23 files, +4023/−0 · php +3954 · yml +69
  • Diff: 2960469
  • Discussion: #3591629 · 2 contributors · 12 comments

Summary

A new optional submodule, entity_blueprint_config, has been added to the Entity Blueprint project. Enabling it extends Entity Blueprint so that config entities (node types, field storage, views, etc.) can be serialized to a blueprint array, deserialized and created or updated from that array, validated structurally and semantically, and manipulated through targeted operations, all using the same patterns as the existing content-entity backend. The module is marked experimental and requires Drupal 10.3 or 11.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New entity_blueprint_config submodule adds ConfigBlueprintBackend, ConfigSerializer, ConfigDeserializer, ConfigOperations (ConfigOperationsInterface), ConfigSchemaBuilder, and the PropertyHandlerInterface / PropertyHandlerManager extension point for serializing config entity properties.

Technical details

The submodule registers a ConfigBlueprintBackend service that implements BlueprintBackendInterface at priority 0 (above the content backend at priority -10). Its supports() method checks that the entity type implements ConfigEntityInterface, so it is selected automatically for all config entity types without any caller changes.

Serialization is handled by ConfigSerializer, which calls $entity->toArray(), strips system properties (uuid, _core, dependencies, langcode) via ConfigEntityMetadataTrait::$systemProperties, and delegates plugin collection keys to PluginCollectionPropertyHandler. Deserialization is two-phase: ConfigStructuralValidator checks required fields and immutability constraints first, then ConfigDeserializer::hydrateEntity() calls $storage->create() or $storage->load() and applies properties; for entities implementing EntityWithPluginCollectionInterface it routes plugin data through DefaultLazyPluginCollection. ConfigSchemaBuilder introspects type definitions from TypedConfigManagerInterface, trying progressively more wildcard segments (up to four) to handle config prefixes with varying dot depth.

The ConfigOperations class (implementing ConfigOperationsInterface) provides targeted mutation methods: updateProperties, addPlugin, updatePlugin, removePlugin, reorderPlugins, and batchOperations. Batch operations apply a list of {op, ...} entries sequentially via a match dispatch, short-circuiting on the first failure. All methods return a ConfigBlueprintResult carrying the unsaved entity on success or collected BlueprintError objects on failure; persistence is the caller's responsibility.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 5, 2026
  • Committed: May 22, 2026 (14 days later)

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • f0ns
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591600: Add infrastructure to support finding commonalities in a set of scalar prop expressions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 24 files, +379/−825 · php +193/−364 · js +98/−362 · yml +88/−99
  • Diff: 173b37d
  • Discussion: #3591600 · 2 contributors · 5 comments

Summary

The ai_plus module replaced its HTML injection pattern for communicating tool results back to the browser with a proper Drupal AJAX command pipeline. Previously, AI tool actions queued raw HTML strings (for example, open-link anchor tags) that were injected directly into DeepChat messages. Now, tool actions queue typed CommandInterface objects through a UiCommandQueue service. These commands are serialized as a drupal_commands key in the DeepChat API JSON response and executed by the browser using Drupal's standard AJAX command system. The polling and instant-refresh auto-reload mechanism is also replaced by new RefreshComponentsController and DeferredProcessingController endpoints. The module-level config setting refresh_mode is removed and replaced by placeholder_media_id.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: ai_plus.settings key refresh_mode (string) removed; replaced by placeholder_media_id (nullable integer)
  • API change: ChatResponseQueue::add(string $html) replaced by UiCommandQueue::add(CommandInterface $command) and UiCommandQueue::addRendered(array $command)

Technical details

The old ChatResponseQueue service accumulated raw HTML strings; DeepChatMessagePrepender (via hook_deepchat_prepend_message) flushed them into chat response text. The "open link" action specifically injected <a class="ai-plus-open-link"> HTML that a MutationObserver in the DeepChat shadow DOM would detect and process.

The new design introduces UiCommandQueue, which accepts CommandInterface instances via add() or pre-rendered arrays via addRendered(). A new DeepChatResponseListener event subscriber listens on KernelEvents::RESPONSE, targets only the ai_chatbot.api route, and injects the rendered command list as drupal_commands into the outgoing JSON. The client-side ai-plus-command-processor.js wraps DeepChat's responseInterceptor to extract drupal_commands and execute them through Drupal.ajax().success(). The open-link action becomes the new OpenLinkCommand PHP class (renders {'command': 'aiPlusOpenLink', 'url': ...}) handled by Drupal.AjaxCommands.prototype.aiPlusOpenLink in ai-plus-open-link.js.

The auto-refresh subsystem is overhauled: ChangeCheckController and the ai_plus.change_check route are removed; ai_plus.refresh_components (POST) replaces them via RefreshComponentsController. Two new routes ai_plus.deferred_processing and ai_plus.deferred_retry are added for DeferredProcessingController. A DeferredProcessorManager plugin collector and ImageGenerationProcessor handle async image-generation tasks. The ai_plus_post_update_add_refresh_mode update hook is deleted. JavaScript files are now injected into the ai_chatbot/deepchat library via hook_library_info_alter rather than through a standalone open_link library. A new Drupal.AiPlus.log() utility (ai-plus-logging.js) enables opt-in debug logging via localStorage.

Upgrade

1. Replace ChatResponseQueue calls with UiCommandQueue:

Old code:

// Injected raw HTML into the chat response.
$this->chatResponseQueue->add('<a href="' . $url . '" class="ai-plus-open-link">' . $title . '</a>');

New code:

use Drupal\ai_plus\Ajax\OpenLinkCommand;

// Queue a typed AJAX command instead.
$this->uiCommandQueue->add(new OpenLinkCommand($url, $title));

2. Config schema change in ai_plus.settings:

Old:

refresh_mode: polling

New:

placeholder_media_id: 1

Remove any code reading ai_plus.settings:refresh_mode. The ai_plus_post_update_add_refresh_mode update hook has been deleted, so sites that have not yet run it will skip it cleanly.

3. Removed routes and libraries:

  • ai_plus.change_check route no longer exists; replace references with ai_plus.refresh_components (POST) or the new deferred routes.
  • The ai_plus/open_link Drupal library is removed; any explicit #attached references to it must be dropped.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 28, 2026
  • Committed: May 22, 2026 (5 days later)

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591028: Consistently use JSON-Schema Draft-07 dialect in frontend and backend by updating `justinrainbow/json-schema` to ^6.8.0
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +32/−9 · js +26/−8 · php +5 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: e92d240
  • Discussion: #3591028 · 2 contributors · 6 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas module now validates component prop schemas using JSON Schema Draft-07 on both the PHP backend and the JavaScript frontend. Previously the PHP backend relied on the justinrainbow/json-schema library which was limited to Draft-04, while the frontend Ajv validator used a different default dialect. This mismatch meant Draft-07 schema features could be silently ignored or produce inconsistent validation results depending on which side evaluated the schema. Both validators are now explicitly configured to the same Draft-07 dialect.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Requirements: Minimum justinrainbow/json-schema version raised from ^6.6.2 to ^6.8.0

Technical details

On the PHP side, CanvasServiceProvider now calls $factory->addMethodCall('setDefaultDialect', [DraftIdentifiers::DRAFT_7]) on the JsonSchema\Constraints\Factory service definition. This requires the bumped justinrainbow/json-schema ^6.8.0 dependency in composer.json (previously ^6.6.2), which is the minimum version that introduced Draft-07 support.

On the JavaScript side, a new createAjv() factory function in ui/src/utils/ajv.ts centralizes Ajv instantiation. It creates an Ajv instance with defaultMeta explicitly set to http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#, making Draft-07 the active dialect for any schema that does not declare a $schema keyword. The ajv-formats-draft2019 package is still layered on top via addDraft2019() to register extended string format validators (like idn-email, iri, duration) without changing the core dialect. Both the component prop-form validation path and InputBehaviors now call createAjv() instead of creating their own ad-hoc new Ajv() instances.

The issue notes this mirrors the approach Drupal core is taking in #3352063 for Single Directory Component prop schemas.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 20, 2026
  • Committed: May 21, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585327: Add HTTP API endpoint for canvasData.v0 to support useSiteData() in Workbench
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +299/−6 · php +115 · js +98/−6 · yml +85 · json +1
  • Diff: 3753845
  • Discussion: #3585327 · 3 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The Canvas module now exposes a new HTTP API endpoint, GET /canvas/api/v0/site-data, that returns the site-level canvasData.v0 payload (base URL, branding, theme assets, and JSON:API settings). Previously, this data was only available when Drupal rendered a full page with the relevant asset libraries attached, which meant Canvas Workbench running as a local Vite dev server had no way to call getSiteData() with live site data. With this change, the @drupal-canvas/vite-plugin fetches the endpoint at build start using an OAuth Bearer token and injects the response into window.drupalSettings.canvasData.v0 before any component code runs, so the existing getSiteData() function works in Workbench without modification. The scope is intentionally limited to site-level data; page-level data (breadcrumbs, page title, main entity) is deferred to a follow-up issue.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New HTTP endpoint GET /canvas/api/v0/site-data returns the site-level canvasData.v0 payload (baseUrl, branding, themeAssets, jsonapiSettings), authenticated via Canvas OAuth.

Technical details

The new route canvas.api.site_data is registered at GET /canvas/api/v0/site-data in canvas.routing.yml with the same access requirements as other read-only Canvas API routes: _canvas_authentication_required: TRUE, _canvas_ui_access: 'TRUE', and canvas_external_api: true. The controller Drupal\canvas\Controller\SiteDataController (marked @internal) calls the four CodeComponentDataProvider methods already used by ComponentSourceHooks::jsSettingsAlter() (getCanvasDataThemeAssetsV0, getCanvasDataJsonApiSettingsV0, getCanvasDataBrandingV0, getCanvasDataBaseUrlV0) and merges them via NestedArray::mergeDeep. It returns a CacheableJsonResponse with cache contexts for url.site and dependencies on system.site, system.theme, system.theme.global, and the active theme's settings config.

OAuth coverage is added by appending canvas.api.site_data to the $named_routes array in CanvasOauthAuthenticationProvider::applies(), which explicitly enumerates routes; without this the OAuth provider would not authenticate requests to the new route.

On the TypeScript side, @drupal-canvas/vite-plugin gains a buildStart() hook that reads an access token (first from the CANVAS_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable, then from the token store via getTokenEntry() from @drupal-canvas/auth). If a valid token is found, it fetches the endpoint and caches the response in canvasApiData. The transformIndexHtml hook then merges canvasApiData into the canvasData.v0 object, resolving relative theme asset paths (logo and favicon URLs starting with /) to absolute URLs by prepending the site base URL. When canvasApiData is present, the static jsonapiPrefix option and CANVAS_JSONAPI_PREFIX env var are no longer used, since jsonapiSettings comes from the API response. The endpoint shape is also added to the module's OpenAPI spec.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 16, 2026
  • Committed: May 15, 2026 (29 days later)

Key contributors

  • mglaman (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)

Collaboration

effulgentsia suggested narrowing the scope to site data only, pointing out that page data is more complex because it requires knowing which page is being requested and should usually be mocked in Workbench rather than fetched live. mglaman accepted the scope reduction and reworked the implementation accordingly, with the page-data endpoint deferred to a follow-up.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
